title: How to Calculate Your Own GPA (And Predict Your Final Semester Grades)
author: Tklesson Team
content:
You wait for your report card. It arrives. Your GPA is lower than you expected. You're confused. You did well in most classes. What happened? You don't have to wait. You can calculate your own GPA at any time. Here is how to do it and predict your final semester grade before the report card arrives.

The Grade Point Scale

Every letter grade has a number value. Most schools use this standard scale:

A = 4.0
A- = 3.7
B+ = 3.3
B = 3.0
B- = 2.7
C+ = 2.3
C = 2.0
C- = 1.7
D+ = 1.3
D = 1.0
D- = 0.7
F = 0.0

Some schools use plus/minus. Some don't. Check your school's policy. If they don't use plus/minus, an A is 4.0, B is 3.0, C is 2.0, etc.

Unweighted vs. Weighted GPA

Unweighted GPA treats all classes the same. An A in gym is worth the same as an A in AP Physics. This is what colleges recalculate.

Weighted GPA gives extra points for harder classes. AP classes might get +1.0 (A=5.0). Honors might get +0.5 (A=4.5). Your school probably reports weighted GPA on your transcript.

When colleges recalculate, they usually unweight your GPA. They want to compare you to other students on the same scale.

How to Calculate Your Unweighted GPA

Step 1: List all your classes. Write the letter grade for each.

Step 2: Convert each letter grade to a number using the scale above.

Step 3: Add all the numbers together.

Step 4: Divide by the number of classes.

Example: You have English (A=4.0), Math (B=3.0), History (A=4.0), Science (B+=3.3), Spanish (A-=3.7). Total = 18.0. Divide by 5 classes = 3.6 GPA.

How to Calculate Weighted GPA

Same process, but add extra points for advanced classes.

If your school gives +1 for AP: AP English A = 5.0 (not 4.0). AP History A = 5.0. Regular Math B = 3.0. Total = 13.0. Divide by 3 = 4.33 weighted GPA.

Check your school's weighting policy. Some give +0.5 for honors. Some give +1 for AP and dual enrollment. Some give nothing for honors. Ask your counselor.

How to Predict Your Final Semester Grade

You know your current grades. You know what's left: final exam, final project, or final paper. You can predict your final grade.

Formula: (Current Grade × Current Weight) + (Final Grade × Final Weight) = Final Course Grade.

Example: Your grade is 85% so far. That's worth 80% of your final grade. The final exam is worth 20%. If you score 90% on the final: (85 × 0.8) + (90 × 0.2) = 68 + 18 = 86%. Your final grade will be 86%.

If you need a certain final grade, solve backwards: What score do I need on the final to get an A? If you have 85% and the final is worth 20%, you need (90 - 68) / 0.2 = 110% on the final. Impossible. Adjust your goal.

Track Your GPA Throughout the Semester

Create a spreadsheet. Update it after every graded assignment. You'll know your standing at all times. No surprises.

This also helps you prioritize. If you have a 97% in one class and 79% in another, spend your study time on the 79% class. The marginal gain is higher.

Calculate your GPA every week. It takes five minutes. You'll never be surprised by your report card again.
